explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: Policies mention automated technologies, inference systems, and vendor oversight, referencing decision-making and data handling policies. Reports detail participation in advisory boards for technology evaluation, investment in and oversight of AI-related systems, and collaborations with technology providers and institutions. An interview highlights a structured approach to data consolidation for AI-driven decision-making, a strategic approach to AI investment and prioritization, and mentions regular meetings with leadership regarding automation and Generative AI.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This Kinder Morgan California Privacy Policy document provides evidence for the **governance** and **privacy** pillars of responsible AI. It supports governance by mentioning automated technologies, inference systems, and vendor oversight, indicating policies around decision-making and data handling. The policy also supports the privacy pillar by addressing data collected by automated systems, sensitive data, deidentification, and third-party analytics use, demonstrating a focus on data protection.
relevant_sources.title: Kinder Morgan California Privacy Policy

relevant_sources.summary: The "Kinder Morgan 2024 Sustainability Report" provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. The report details Kinder Morgan's participation in advisory boards for technology evaluation, their investment in and oversight of AI-related systems like automated visual inspection, and their collaborations with technology providers and institutions. These activities demonstrate mechanisms for governance and oversight in the development and deployment of AI technologies.
relevant_sources.title: Kinder Morgan 2024 Sustainability Report

relevant_sources.summary: This interview with Kinder Morgan's CFO, David Michels, provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. The discussion highlights a structured approach to data consolidation for AI-driven decision-making and a strategic approach to AI investment and prioritization, indicating governance over data infrastructure and AI initiatives. Furthermore, mentions of automation and Generative AI, coupled with regular meetings with the CIO and leadership team, imply operational governance and oversight for AI deployment.
relevant_sources.title: Kinder Morgan: Navigating growth in the digital energy era

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.findings: A privacy policy addresses data collected by automated systems, sensitive data, and deidentification. The policy also addresses the use of third-party analytics.
privacy.path_to_improvement: Publish AI-specific privacy impact assessments or data handling procedures.

relevant_sources.summary: This Kinder Morgan California Privacy Policy document provides evidence for the **governance** and **privacy** pillars of responsible AI. It supports governance by mentioning automated technologies, inference systems, and vendor oversight, indicating policies around decision-making and data handling. The policy also supports the privacy pillar by addressing data collected by automated systems, sensitive data, deidentification, and third-party analytics use, demonstrating a focus on data protection.
relevant_sources.title: Kinder Morgan California Privacy Policy

transparency.path_to_improvement: Document AI systems in use, including vendor/third-party AI tools.

summary.overall_findings: Kinder Morgan's public disclosures include a privacy policy that addresses data collected by automated systems and sensitive data, alongside governance policies that mention automated technologies and inference systems. These materials address 2 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars, with the assessment drawing on 12 publicly available sources. No qualifying public evidence was found for the majority of the 7 evaluated pillars.
